edges.evidence: D53 was shown to interact with D14 by pull-down assay. Interaction enhanced by GR24 in a dose-dependent manner
D14- and D3-dependent: SLs induce D53 degradation by the proteasome and abrogate its activity in promoting axillary bud outgrowth
SCF(D3) ubiquitinates D53, then degraded via 26S proteasome
D53 physically interacts with IPA1 in vivo and in vitro and suppresses the transcriptional activation activity of IPA1
D53 represses OsCKX9; knockdown of D53 increases OsCKX9 expression
IPA1 directly binds to D53 promoter GTAC element and activates D53 transcription, forming negative feedback loop
IPA1 could directly bind to the promoter of rice TEOSINTE BRANCHED1 via GTAC motif binding to suppress rice tillering
IPA1 can directly bind to the promoter of DEP1 at different sites with GTAC core motifs
EMSA assays confirmed that IPA1 could directly target the promoter or first intron of LOG
Direct EMSA confirmation for SLR1 promoter targeting
EMSA confirmed IPA1 could directly target PIN1b promoter
ChIP-seq binding site identified in OsGID2 promoter region
OsmiR156 regulates OsSPL14 by targeting miR156 binding site; one nucleotide substitution perturbs OsmiR156-directed mRNA degradation
miR529a regulates OsSPL14/IPA1 cleaving the mRNA alongside miR156
2'-epi-5-deoxystrigol was undetectable in d27 vs. wild-type root exudates; D27 is iron-containing isomerase
D10 encodes CCD8 that cleaves 9-cis-beta-carotene to carlactone
D17/HTD1 encodes CCD7 that acts on 9-cis-beta-carotene
Auxin up-regulates D10 expression via AXR1-TIR1 pathway
auxin induces expression of D17 SL biosynthesis gene
D53 interacts with MOC1 and represses MOC1 activity in tiller regulation
TAD1 interacts directly with MOC1; TAD1 recruits MOC1 to APC/C and degrades MOC1 cell-cycle-dependently; MOC1 accumulated to a higher level in tad1
TE-APC/C mediates degradation of MOC1 by ubiquitin-26S proteasome; te mutant shows drastically increased tiller number
MOC1 is protected from degradation by binding to the DELLA protein SLR1; cell-free protein degradation assay showed degradation of MBP-MOC1 was inhibited by His-Trx-SLR1
GAs trigger the degradation of SLR1, leading to stem elongation
MOC1 regulates LAX1 expression in axillary bud formation network
TE-OE lines had reduced OSH1 expression via MOC1 degradation; MOC1 positively regulates OSH1
MOC1 plays an essential role in the initiation of axillary bud formation; moc1 mutant monoculm without tillers
Physical interaction between MOC1 and MOC3 (TAB1) up-regulates FON1 expression
physical interaction between MOC1 and MOC3 can up-regulate the expression of FON1
TAB1 is required for the proper expression of OSH1 in premeristem zone
FON2 restricts the stem cell population by moderately repressing TAB1 expression
LAX1 plays essential role in axillary meristem formation by genetically functioning together with LAX2 and MOC1
LAX2 acts in maintenance of axillary meristem
Not direct; OsMADS57 regulates D14 negatively
OsMADS57 bound to the CArG motif in the D14 promoter and directly suppressed D14 expression
Physical interaction reduces OsMADS57 inhibition of D14 (OsTB1 relieves OsMADS57 repression of D14)
miRNA444a targets OsMADS57 mRNA for degradation
SL can induce the expression of OsCKX9 to down-regulate CK content; GR24 treatment activates OsCKX9
OsCKX9 functions as CK catabolic enzyme, degrading cytokinin
OsRR5 is significantly repressed after 4h of GR24 treatment (reflects CK depletion)
IPI1 promotes the degradation of IPA1 in panicles via K48-linked polyubiquitination; in shoot apex K63 chains stabilize IPA1
OsSHI1 repressed the transcriptional activation activity of IPA1 via physical interaction; OsSHI1 reduces IPA1 DNA binding affinity
Cytokinin binds OsHK4 histidine kinase receptor activating downstream phospho-relay
OsHK4 phosphorylates OsRR21 via AHP; OsRR21 phosphorylation response to CK diminished in pal1 mutant
OsRR21 directly activates IPA1 by binding to 10 CK response elements in IPA1 promoter
IPA1 upregulates OsHK4 via interactions with TCP TFs binding TGGGCC motifs in OsHK4 promoter forming positive feedback
OsCCA1 directly binds CBS elements in OsTB1 promoter activating expression
OsCCA1 directly binds CBS in D14 promoter; ChIP-qPCR confirmed
OsCCA1 directly binds CBS in D10 promoter
OsCCA1 directly binds CBS/EE elements in IPA1 promoter
OsCCA1 directly binds CBS on OsPRR1 promoter (negative regulation in reciprocal feedback)
OsPRR1 forms reciprocal feedback loop with OsCCA1 (negative repression)
Photosynthetic sugars negatively regulate OsCCA1 expression in roots/buds
Sucrose promotes D53 accumulation (protein stability) and tillering in rice
OsNLP4 RWP-RK domain binds NRE motif in OsNiR promoter enhancing transcription
Nitrate triggers nuclear retention of NLP4 and transcription activation
OsTCP19 directly represses DLT expression; inhibits N-regulated tillering
DLT is an important BR-signaling gene to promote tiller outgrowth
N-induced OsLBD37/OsLBD39 directly inhibit OsTCP19 activity
N-induced OsLBD37/OsLBD39 directly inhibit OsTCP19 activity
Nitrogen supply (nitrate) promotes NGR5 accumulation and PRC2 recruitment to branching-inhibitor genes
NGR5-PRC2 complex directly represses OsTB1 via H3K27me3
NGR5 recruits PRC2 to D14 for H3K27me3 repression
GA-GID1 complex targets NGR5 for proteasomal degradation
SLR1 competes with NGR5 for GID1 binding, preventing NGR5 degradation
High nitrogen represses OsTCP19 expression via LBD37/39
NPF7.7 transports nitrate promoting tillers
OsAAP5 affects rice tiller number by regulating cytokinin biosynthesis
OsBZR1 recruits D53 to repress OsTB1 expression forming D53-BZR1 complex
SL treatment triggers degradation of OsBZR1
BR stabilizes OsBZR1 protein
OsTB1 positively regulates expression of D14 (via OsMADS57 complex)

perturbations.evidence: d53 mutant shows ~3x WT tiller number; SL-insensitive dominant allele
pActin::D53-GFP causes moderate increase in tillering
d14 mutant SL-insensitive, high tiller number, dwarf
d14 + GR24 treatment: axillary buds still grow (SL-insensitive)
d3 + 10uM GR24: tillers not suppressed; SL-insensitive
fc1 + 10uM GR24: third and fourth leaf tiller buds were not affected
d10 + 1uM GR24: GR24 suppressed tiller bud outgrowth (SL-deficient mutant responds)
d27 + 1.0uM GR24 fully rescues tillering dwarf phenotype
d27 final tiller number three times wild-type; SL-deficient
WT + 10uM GR24 completely inhibited outgrowth of third leaf tiller buds
35S-FC1/d3-2 partial rescue of d3 phenotype (reduced tiller, plant height)
BAP cytokinin treatment suppresses FC1 expression and promotes axillary bud outgrowth
tad1 showed increased tiller number, reduced plant height; MOC1 accumulates
TAD1-overexpressing plants show reduced tiller number; MOC1 reduced
te mutant displays drastically increased tiller number (~3x WT)
TE-OE reduced tiller number, accelerated MOC1 degradation
te moc1-5 double: reduced tillers like moc1-5 alone; MOC1 epistatic
SLR1-RNAi: fewer tillers, taller stature
SLR1-OE: increased tiller number, reduced height
MOC1-OE: increased number of axillary buds and tillers
GA 10^-5 M 1 month treatment decreased tiller number
Paclobutrazol (GA inhibitor) increased tiller number and decreased plant height
ipa1-1D (miR156 site mutation): IPA1 accumulation, reduced tiller number, more panicle branches
ipa1-10 loss-of-function: more tillers, smaller panicles
ipa1-1D could significantly suppress the high tillering phenotype of d53
ipa1-1D could repress the high-tillering phenotype of d27
ipi1-1 CRISPR mutant: reduced IPA1 in shoot apex, increased tillers
Ub:IPI1 OE: increased IPA1 in shoot apex, reduced tillers
shi1 mutant: dramatically reduced tiller number, increased panicle branches
OsSHI1-OE: reduced plant height, decreased panicle, ectopic tillers at upper internodes
osmads57-1 activation-tagged: +28 tillers vs WT ~10
OsMADS57-OE: increased tillers; suppresses D14
OsMIR444a-OE: suppresses OsMADS57, fewer tillers
osckx9 mutant: increased tillers, decreased plant height and panicle size
OsCKX9-OX: reduced plant height, decreased panicle branches; Note: SL-induced via D53 repression
tab1-1 splice mutant: no tillers even in 2-month-old shoots
oscca1 mutant shows more tillers and delayed flowering
OsCCA1-OE: fewer tillers than transgenic control
OsPRR1-OE: reduced tiller numbers
ohk4 loss-of-function: reduced panicle length, primary branches, secondary branches; higher-order tillers reduced
osnlp4 mutant: decreased tiller number and plant height
ngr5 + high nitrogen: no tiller response to N (insensitive)
